title: Lumi CUI: IKEA-minimal visual redesign + desktop split layout
description:
Two-part redesign of the chat UI in src/App.tsx.

P1 — Visual redesign (IKEA-inspired):
- Replace current pastel/glassmorphism aesthetic with modern industrial, minimal IKEA design language
- Clean, flat surfaces; no ambient gradient blobs, no heavy backdrop-blur glass
- Tight geometry, neutral palette (warm off-whites, greys, a single accent — think IKEA blue/yellow used sparingly, or pure monochrome with one accent)
- Crisp typography; consider switching from Space Grotesk to a more industrial/neutral sans (e.g., Inter, IBM Plex Sans, or keep a minimal grotesk but flatten the styling)
- Flatten buttons, inputs, product cards, header, modal — reduce shadows, rounded corners kept modest, borders thin and defined
- Must apply consistently across: header, chat bubbles, quick replies, product cards, product detail modal, input bar, typing indicator

P2 — Desktop responsive layout:
- Mobile (<768px or similar breakpoint): unchanged layout — identical to current single-column chat experience
- Desktop: two-column split
  - RIGHT side: the mobile-like vertical chat column, constrained to a phone-like max width (e.g., 420–480px), pinned to the right of the viewport with full height
  - LEFT side: a gallery/canvas area that shows furniture image result cards related to the assistant's answers. When the assistant surfaces products (currently rendered inline inside AssistantReply), on desktop those cards should appear in the left panel instead of (or in addition to) the chat column
  - The left panel should feel like a showroom/moodboard — larger imagery, IKEA-style clean grid
  - When no products have been recommended yet, the left panel can show an empty state (muted, minimal — e.g., a hint like 'Your selections will appear here')

Acceptance:
- npm run dev runs cleanly, no TS errors (npm run lint passes)
- Visual check in browser at both mobile (~375px) and desktop (~1440px) widths
- Mobile layout is visually indistinguishable from desktop's right column in structure
- Product recommendations appear in the left gallery on desktop and inline on mobile
- No regressions to the conversation flow, Gemini calls, or state machine

Files expected to change: src/App.tsx (primary), possibly index.html (font swap), possibly src/index.css
closeReason: IKEA-minimal visual redesign + desktop split layout implemented. P1: flat surfaces, #0058A3 accent, Inter font, no glassmorphism. P2: left gallery panel + right 440px chat column on desktop, mobile unchanged. tsc passes clean.
